title: The canonical KdV conservative update preserves discrete mass under periodic flux

source.notes: Canonical KdV specialization using flux coefficient 3 and dispersion coefficient 1.

informal.statement: For the canonical KdV-like flux 3*u_i^2 + secondForwardDifference(u)_i, periodic flux implies that the canonical conservative update preserves discrete mass.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state
n is the number of cells in the finite range
informal.expected_conclusion: The canonical KdV conservative update preserves discrete mass under periodic flux.

declarations.name: kdvCanonicalFlux
declarations.statement: def kdvCanonicalFlux (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := kdvConservativeFlux 3 1 u i

declarations.name: kdvCanonicalUpdate
declarations.statement: def kdvCanonicalUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := kdvConservativeUpdate scale 3 1 u i

declarations.name: discreteMass_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_periodicFlux
declarations.statement: theorem discreteMass_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_periodicFlux (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary (kdvCanonicalFlux u) n) : discreteMass (fun i => kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u i) n = discreteMass u n

tasks.input: Translate the canonical KdV periodic-flux discrete-mass preservation theorem into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem discreteMass_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_periodicFlux (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : periodicBoundary (kdvCanonicalFlux u) n) : discreteMass (fun i => kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u i) n = discreteMass u n

tasks.input: Given the configurable KdV mass-preservation theorem, complete the canonical specialization proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding kdvCanonicalUpdate and converting the periodic-flux hypothesis with kdvCanonicalFlux.

tasks.input: Find the configurable KdV theorem used to prove the canonical KdV mass-preservation theorem.
